concerns.text: Phase 6 tasks (T040-T047) introduce 'Reviewer Response' work that mandates generating new experimental data (e.g., T045 '0.01 angstrom precision' checks, T042 'thermodynamic confounders' extraction) or performing rule enumeration experiments (T046) not defined in the spec's User Stories or Functional Requirements. This violates Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) and Principle VI (Open Crystallographic Data Integrity) by requiring data sources outside the COD or non-reproducible manual steps, and constitutes scope creep that relaxes the computational-only constraint of the spec.

Task T027 instructs using a '[deferred]' number of shuffles for the permutation test. The spec (FR-016) and Constitution Principle VII explicitly mandate a fixed 10,000 shuffles to achieve 0.0001 resolution. Using a deferred/variable count silently weakens the statistical rigor requirement and fails to satisfy the acceptance criteria in US-2.

Task T028 suggests implementing a 'block-wise VIF approach' or omitting raw dimensions if fingerprint dimensionality prevents standard VIF. FR-009 explicitly requires VIF on 'all predictor variables' without omitting raw dimensions or defaulting to PCA without explicit justification. This task description relaxes the mandatory diagnostic requirement.

Task T045 requires validating predictions against '0.01 angstrom precision' typical of crystallographic measurements. The spec and plan rely solely on downloaded CIFs and generated SMILES; the raw diffraction intensity profiles or high-precision measurement logs required for such a check are not available in the COD dataset. This task demands an impossible verification step not supported by the data source defined in FR-017 and Constitution Principle VI.

Tasks T046 and T047 mandate a 'rule enumeration experiment' and comparative analysis against the MLP. These are not defined in the User Stories, Functional Requirements, or Success Criteria. Implementing them adds undefined complexity and scope creep, violating the constraint that tasks must strictly satisfy the spec's defined requirements.
